plan.annotation: Source: Portable runtime contract (architecture-contract). Validation runs before commit; three named adapters sit behind the adapter boundary.

operations.label: Editable nodes: Client, Portable NodeSlide Protocol (initialize, stage, commit, restore), Validation Engine, Adapter Boundary, Memory Adapter, Convex Adapter, Postgres Adapter
operations.value: 7 native editable nodes in a single left-to-right reading direction

operations.label: Typed connectors: calls (Client to Protocol), validates-before-commit (Protocol to Validation Engine), persists (Validation Engine to each adapter via boundary)
operations.value: Labeled typed connectors linking protocol, validation, and the three named adapters

operations.label: Trust boundary: visually distinct adapter boundary separating portable protocol and validation from backend-specific persistence adapters
operations.value: 1 visibly distinct trust boundary around the adapter zone

plan.pptxFallback: PowerPoint export uses grouped editable shapes and native connectors for the client, protocol, validation engine, adapter boundary, and three adapters; the trust boundary is a distinct editable outline. Nodes and connectors remain editable; the architecture is never replaced with paragraph arrows.
plan.takeaway: Client calls flow through a portable protocol and validation engine before commit, with memory, Convex, and Postgres adapters implementing persistence behind a distinct adapter trust boundary.
plan.title: Portable Protocol & Adapter Boundary
